title: Docker Backend: Remove the per-session Docker sidecar implementation
description:
## Problem

The superseded per-session Docker-in-Docker architecture remains implemented and supported across runtime schemas, session orchestration, worker registration, preflight, settings, status, credentials, cleanup, and tests.

This is not a dormant compatibility flag:

- `moonmind/schemas/agent_runtime_models.py` validates a `docker-sidecar` capability, `DOCKER_HOST`, DinD image, graph/socket volumes, resources, and cleanup policy;
- `moonmind/schemas/managed_session_models.py` persists sidecar capability/status data;
- `moonmind/workflows/temporal/runtime/managed_session_controller.py` creates/restarts `docker:27-dind`, injects a session endpoint, probes Docker/Compose, manages Docker config, and reaps sidecar containers/volumes;
- `moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_catalog.py` registers `agent_runtime.ensure_docker_sidecar`;
- `moonmind/workflows/temporal/worker_runtime.py` constructs/registers sidecar controllers and startup reconciliation;
- `moonmind/agents/codex_worker/cli.py` and `moonmind/workflows/automation/preflight.py` enforce sidecar/Docker readiness;
- compose settings, GHCR/Unreal preflight variables, tests, status/profile fields, and temporary docs still refer to the sidecar.

Keeping this code after the canonical container-job path lands would preserve two conflicting architectures, retain raw daemon authority inside managed sessions, and prevent reliable shared-image semantics.

## Desired behavior

After managed-session callers have cut over to #3261 and owned job cleanup exists in #3263, remove the per-session sidecar as a supported mode. Managed sessions use MoonMind's authenticated container-job service and never receive a Docker socket, Docker endpoint, or `DOCKER_HOST`.

No compatibility mode remains simply because MoonMind is pre-release. Add a narrowly bounded Temporal replay/cutover guard only if repository/runtime evidence proves active histories or persisted payloads require one, and document the evidence and removal condition. Legacy sidecar containers/volumes may be removed once through a conservative ownership-checked cleanup path; shared images are not pruned.

## Current evidence

- `moonmind/schemas/agent_runtime_models.py` contains sidecar image, mode, socket, graph, resource, cleanup, and environment invariants, plus unsupported/speculative mode vocabulary under the same capability.
- `moonmind/schemas/managed_session_models.py` exposes sidecar readiness/status fields as part of managed-session state.
- `moonmind/workflows/temporal/runtime/managed_session_controller.py` contains sidecar request construction, lifecycle, probes, Docker config, volume naming, restart, and orphan reaping.
- `moonmind/workflows/temporal/activity_catalog.py` and `worker_runtime.py` register and construct the sidecar Activity/controller.
- `moonmind/agents/codex_worker/cli.py` invokes the preflight; `moonmind/workflows/automation/preflight.py` requires Docker CLI/sidecar and performs optional image manifest checks.
- `docker-compose.yaml` injects `GHCR_PULL_USER`, `GHCR_PULL_TOKEN`, Unreal image/preflight variables, Docker proxy details, and worker dependencies/comments partly for sidecar use.
- Sidecar-specific coverage exists in schema, controller, preflight, managed-runtime profile, GHCR auth, Codex session runtime, and integration tests (including files returned by repository search for `docker-sidecar`/`ensure_docker_sidecar`).
- `docs/ManagedAgents/DockerSidecarRuntime.md` and `DockerOutOfDocker.md` are tombstones/consolidation pointers, not authority to preserve the implementation.

## Scope

- Complete a repository-wide dependency inventory for sidecar-only code, settings, schemas, serialized payloads, tests, images, volumes, status fields, docs, and CLI packages before deletion.
- Remove the sidecar capability/model surface from:
  - managed runtime profiles and validators;
  - managed-session status/capability snapshots;
  - API schemas and UI/status fields where present;
  - runtime mode enums and unsupported speculative alternatives that exist only under the obsolete sidecar capability.
- Remove sidecar lifecycle implementation from `managed_session_controller.py`, including:
  - image/container creation and restart;
  - graph/socket volume creation;
  - Docker config/auth materialization;
  - readiness and Compose probes;
  - `DOCKER_HOST` injection;
  - sidecar-specific orphan/reaper logic.
- Remove `agent_runtime.ensure_docker_sidecar` Activity contracts, catalog registration, handlers, task routing, and worker construction/startup reconciliation.
- Remove sidecar preflight from Codex/managed-runtime startup and remove Docker CLI/Compose requirements that exist solely so the agent can drive its own daemon.
- Remove or normalize sidecar-only settings/environment variables and compose wiring, including credential/preflight values whose only remaining consumer was session DinD. Preserve settings demonstrably required by the trusted system-Docker backend.
- Remove sidecar-specific tests and replace them with assertions for the canonical `containerJobs` capability and absence of Docker authority.
- Remove temporary implementation docs/status examples that still describe sidecar as supported; retain the two tombstones as consolidation pointers if they continue to serve that role.
- Add a conservative one-time cleanup/reconciliation path for legacy MoonMind-labeled sidecar containers and graph/socket volumes:
  - dry-run/diagnostics first;
  - verify legacy ownership labels/naming and absence of an active managed session;
  - never delete images, unrelated volumes, or unlabeled resources;
  - remove the migration path after the documented cleanup window.
- Audit active Temporal histories and persisted runtime-profile/session payloads:
  - if no concrete replay/cutover dependency exists, remove the code directly;
  - if concrete evidence exists, use the smallest Temporal patch/version/read-old guard needed, document affected history/payload versions, set an expiry/removal condition, and never present the sidecar as a supported runtime option.

## Out of scope

- Restoring DinD, retaining it as an opt-in compatibility mode, or adding a new session-local daemon.
- Implementing Kubernetes, Podman, containerd, a daemon pool, or a dedicated MoonMind daemon.
- Deleting shared Docker images or using global prune.
- Removing Docker tooling from trusted backend worker images when it is still required by #3254.
- Replacing the canonical container-job service with direct system-Docker access from sessions.
- Toolchain-specific replacement infrastructure for Unreal, .NET, Node, Unity, or other workloads.

## Security and authority boundaries

- The MoonMind API authenticates/authorizes container-job requests and session workspace use through #3259/#3261.
- Temporal owns container-job lifecycle and explicit cancellation; managed-session teardown no longer controls Docker resources directly.
- Trusted backend workers retain the configured system-Docker authority needed by #3254.
- Legacy cleanup may delete only positively identified MoonMind-owned sidecar objects after durable session-state checks.
- Managed agents must not receive a Docker socket, Docker API endpoint, `DOCKER_HOST`, sidecar Docker config, graph volume, registry credential, or direct proxy-network access.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Managed runtime/profile schemas no longer expose or validate a supported `docker-sidecar` capability, session-local Docker endpoint, graph/socket volumes, or sidecar resources.
- [ ] Managed-session state/status no longer advertises sidecar readiness or `DOCKER_HOST`; it advertises the canonical `containerJobs` capability from #3261.
- [ ] `managed_session_controller.py` no longer creates, starts, probes, restarts, or reaps DinD sidecars or injects Docker authority into agents.
- [ ] `agent_runtime.ensure_docker_sidecar` and all related Temporal registration/worker construction are removed.
- [ ] Codex/managed-runtime preflight no longer requires a Docker CLI/Compose daemon for session operation.
- [ ] Sidecar-only settings, compose wiring, credentials, image preflight, schemas, tests, status fields, and lifecycle code are removed or demonstrably retained for a non-sidecar trusted dependency.
- [ ] New managed sessions complete generic container jobs through MoonMind MCP/API with no sidecar present.
- [ ] No sidecar compatibility/runtime mode remains user- or operator-selectable.
- [ ] Any replay/payload compatibility code is backed by concrete evidence, version-gated, non-advertised, tested, and carries a documented removal condition; otherwise no wrapper is added.
- [ ] Legacy sidecar containers/volumes can be identified and conservatively removed without deleting images or unrelated resources.
- [ ] Repository search finds no supported sidecar implementation references outside explicit tombstones, migration notes/tests, or narrowly time-bounded replay guards.
- [ ] Omnigent-host and managed-session containers remain without Docker authority.

## Verification

- Repository-wide static/search contract tests for `docker-sidecar`, DinD image names, sidecar Activity names, graph/socket fields, `DOCKER_HOST` injection, sidecar status fields, and obsolete environment variables.
- Schema/API tests proving old sidecar configuration is rejected rather than silently accepted as supported.
- Managed-session integration test proving startup, turns, reset/termination, and a generic container job work without DinD or Docker CLI in the agent runtime.
- Worker registration tests proving no sidecar Activity/controller is loaded.
- Compose/config tests proving only trusted backend workers retain required Docker-proxy access and session/Omnigent containers do not.
- Legacy cleanup tests with owned active, owned inactive, mismatched, and unlabeled containers/volumes; assert no image deletion/prune.
- Temporal replay tests only if the history audit demonstrates a concrete requirement; include the exact historical fixture/version.
- Persisted-profile/session migration tests only if a real payload cutover is required.
- Regression tests replacing sidecar expectations in the existing schema/controller/preflight/GHCR/Codex runtime suites.

## Dependencies

- Parent tracker: #3251.
- Prerequisites: #3261 for managed-session container-job cutover, #3260 for workflow caller cutover, and #3263 for durable-state-aware cleanup/reconciliation.
- Uses #3257 so private-image credentials no longer depend on the sidecar Docker config path.
- Blocks final generic validation in the tracker; sidecar removal must be proven before the architecture is considered converged.
- Supersedes the implementation described by the non-normative `DockerSidecarRuntime.md` tombstone; it does not supersede the canonical `DockerBackendService.md`.

## Documentation authority

- `docs/ManagedAgents/DockerBackendService.md` §§1–3 (no sidecar compatibility path), §§15–16, §19 rule 16, §21 rule 14, and §22.
- `docs/ManagedAgents/DockerSidecarRuntime.md` and `docs/ManagedAgents/DockerOutOfDocker.md` as explicit consolidation/tombstone pointers.
- `docs/ManagedAgents/ManagedAgentArchitecture.md` sections separating session continuity from container workloads.
- `docs/ManagedAgents/CodexCliManagedSessions.md` sections defining session identity independent of container jobs.
- `docs/Temporal/ManagedAndExternalAgentExecutionModel.md` sections governing replay-safe cutover and tool-versus-agent identity.
